title: Renaissance art was a weapon - Ada Palmer
summary: Ada Palmer discusses how Renaissance art functioned as a weapon, presumably in ideological, political, or social contexts. The episode explores the instrumental and strategic dimensions of artistic production during the Renaissance period.
writeup: Ada Palmer argues that Renaissance art operated as a weapon, wielded for ideological, political, and social ends rather than as decoration alone. The conversation with Dwarkesh Patel centers on the instrumental and strategic dimensions of artistic production during the period, treating commissioned works as tools of power and persuasion. The takeaway is a reframing of Renaissance art as an active instrument in contests over influence.
